mission.title: Run llm-d Benchmark Workload
mission.description: Run inference benchmarks against a deployed llm-d stack using the inference-perf tool. Supports multiple benchmark templates for different well-lit paths (inference scheduling, P/D disaggregation, wide EP, precise prefix cache). Results include latency metrics (TTFT, TPOT, ITL), throughput, and standardized benchmark reports.

steps.title: Ensure llm-d stack is deployed
steps.description:
You must have a deployed llm-d inference stack from one of the well-lit path guides:
- [Inference Scheduling](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/inference-scheduling)
- [P/D Disaggregation](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/pd-disaggregation)
- [Wide EP with LWS](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/wide-ep-lws)
- [Precise Prefix Cache](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/precise-prefix-cache-aware)

steps.title: Install prerequisites
steps.description:
Install required tools:
- `yq` (v4+)
- `timeout` utility (macOS: `brew install coreutils`)

Download the benchmark runner script:
```bash
curl -L -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d-benchmark/main/existing_stack/run_only.sh
chmod u+x run_only.sh
```

steps.title: Create a PVC for benchmark results
steps.description:
Create a PersistentVolumeClaim with RWX access mode (200Gi recommended):
```bash
export BENCHMARK_PVC="workload-pvc"
cat <<YAML | kubectl -n ${NAMESPACE} apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: ${BENCHMARK_PVC}
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteMany
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 200Gi
YAML
```

steps.title: Select and configure benchmark template
steps.description:
Download the appropriate benchmark template for your deployment:
```bash
# For inference-scheduling:
curl -LJO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/guides/benchmark/inference_scheduling_guide_template.yaml

# For P/D disaggregation:
curl -LJO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/guides/benchmark/pd_template.yaml

# For precise prefix cache:
curl -LJO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/guides/benchmark/precise_template.yaml
```

Configure the template:
```bash
export NAMESPACE=<your-namespace>
export BENCHMARK_PVC=workload-pvc
export GATEWAY_SVC=<your-gateway-service-name>
envsubst < <template>.yaml > config.yaml
```

steps.title: Run the benchmark
steps.description:
Execute the benchmark:
```bash
./run_only.sh -c config.yaml
```

The script launches a `llmdbench-harness-launcher` pod that runs inference-perf with multiple stages at different request rates. Progress is displayed in the terminal.

steps.title: Review benchmark results
steps.description:
Results are stored on the PVC under `/requests/inference-perf_<experiment_ID>_<workload>_<model>/`. Key output files include:
- `summary_lifecycle_metrics.json` — overall metrics summary
- Per-stage YAML reports with latency percentiles (TTFT, TPOT, ITL)
- Throughput metrics (requests/sec, tokens/sec)

Access results via the launcher pod:
```bash
kubectl exec -it llmdbench-harness-launcher -n ${NAMESPACE} -- ls /requests/
```

resolution.summary: A successful benchmark run completes all stages and produces result files on the PVC including latency metrics, throughput numbers, and standardized benchmark reports in YAML format.

uninstall.title: Remove benchmark pod and PVC
Clean up local files
uninstall.description:
Remove downloaded scripts and configs:
```bash
rm -f run_only.sh config.yaml *_template.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update benchmark tooling
upgrade.description:
Download the latest version of the benchmark runner:
```bash
curl -L -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d-benchmark/main/existing_stack/run_only.sh
chmod u+x run_only.sh
```

Download updated benchmark templates from the latest llm-d release.

troubleshooting.title: Benchmark pod stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
The PVC may not have a matching PersistentVolume. Verify your storage class supports RWX access:
```bash
kubectl describe pvc ${BENCHMARK_PVC} -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

troubleshooting.title: Gateway service not found
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure `GATEWAY_SVC` matches the exact service name in your namespace:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Common names: `infra-inference-scheduling-inference-gateway-istio`, `infra-pd-inference-gateway-istio`.

troubleshooting.title: Benchmark failures at high request rates
troubleshooting.description: Request failures at high rates may indicate the stack is saturated. This is expected behavior — the benchmark sweeps through increasing rates to find the maximum throughput. Review per-stage results to identify the sustainable throughput ceiling.

troubleshooting.title: timeout command not found (macOS)
troubleshooting.description:
On macOS, install the timeout utility:
```bash
brew install coreutils
```

prerequisites.description: A deployed llm-d inference stack, a PersistentVolumeClaim with RWX access (200Gi recommended), and the yq YAML processor (v4+).
